title: Create greet(name) function in greet.js
description: Create file greet.js in project root. Export a function greet(name) that returns the string 'Hello, ' + name + '!'. Use CommonJS (module.exports). Keep entire file under 10 lines. Example: greet('World') should return 'Hello, World!'.

title: Verify greet() function with test cases
description: Test greet.js function. Verify: greet('World') returns 'Hello, World!', greet('Alice') returns 'Hello, Alice!', greet('') returns 'Hello, !'. Test with Node.js require(). Document results in 2-3 sentences — pass/fail only.

title: Add greet() usage to README
description: Append a 'greet() Function' section to README.md. Include: file location (greet.js), usage example (const greet = require('./greet'); console.log(greet('World'));), expected output. Keep to 4-5 lines max.
